sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 4 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 123, level 2.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- Plan: Try DOWN from cols 34-38
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 35 of about 123 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The target is still at rows 40-43, cols 29-33. UP was blocked, LEFT was blocked. This is strange - earlier UP from this position worked (moved to rows 35-39, cols 29-33).
Wait, looking at the output more carefully:
- After LEFT: target at rows 40-41, cols 29-33 (orange) and rows 42-44, cols 29-33 (blue)
- After UP: target still at rows 40-41, cols 29-33 - UP was blocked!
- After 3x LEFT: target still at rows 40-41, cols 29-33 - LEFT was blocked
But earlier, UP from rows 40-43, cols 29-33 worked and moved to rows 35-39, cols 29-33. What changed?
Looking at the history, the target started at rows 40-43, cols 29-33. After 5 UP actions, it moved to rows 35-39, cols 29-33. Then RIGHT moved it to rows 35-39, cols 34-38. Then 6 UP moved it to rows 5-9, cols 34-38. Then LEFT moved it to rows 5-9, cols 29-33, then further LEFT to rows 5-9, cols 19-23. Then 4 DOWN moved it back to rows 40-43, cols 29-33.
But now UP is blocked at rows 40-43, cols 29-33. This suggests the maze state has changed - perhaps some passages closed or the level has a different configuration now.
Wait, I need to reconsider. Maybe the target's position changed but I'm misreading it. Let me look at the segmentation data again:
- After LEFT: O at rows 40-41, cols 29-33
- After UP: O at rows 40-41, cols 29-33 (no change)
- After 3x LEFT: O at rows 40-41, cols 29-33 (no change)
So UP and LEFT are both blocked now. This is different from before. Maybe the maze has changed, or maybe I need to try RIGHT and DOWN.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the level. In Level 1, the goal was to collect all yellow items. Maybe in Level 2, the goal is different - perhaps to navigate to a specific location or collect items in a specific order.
Looking at the current board, I see:
- Yellow items at rows 16-18, cols 15-17 and rows 51-53, cols 40-42
- White item at rows 46-47, cols 51-52
- Blue item at rows 55-60, cols 3-8
Maybe the goal is to collect all these items by navigating to them. Let me check if the yellow items have been collected or if they're still there.
Actually, the yellow items at rows 16-18, cols 15-17 and rows 51-53, cols 40-42 are still present. The white item at rows 46-47, cols 51-52 is also still there.
Let me try RIGHT from the current position to see if that works.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (updated):
- Target at rows 40-43, cols 29-33
- UP and LEFT now blocked (different from earlier!)
- Maze state may have changed. Need to try RIGHT and DOWN
- Plan: Try RIGHT and DOWN to find new paths

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Target at rows 40-43, cols 29-33
# Try RIGHT
result = action(['RIGHT'])
print(f"RIGHT: done={result.get('done')}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('O', 'b', 'W', 'Y', 'R'):
        b = n['boundary']
        if len(b) >= 2:
            rows = [p[0] for p in b]
            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# If RIGHT works, try DOWN
if not result.get('done') and not result.get('level_completed'):
    result2 = action(['DOWN', 'DOWN', 'DOWN', 'DOWN'])
    print(f"4x DOWN: done={result2.get('done')}, level_completed={result2.get('level_completed')}")
    seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
    for n in seg2['nodes']:
        if n['color'] in ('O', 'b', 'W', 'Y', 'R'):
            b = n['boundary']
            if len(b) >= 2:
                rows = [p[0] for p in b]
                cols = [p[1] for p in b]
                print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
